reasoning: I have seen Didi's schemes reach my hands—my cycle, my rice at two rupees, my mother-in-law alive. When I look at Shantanu's CAA promise now, on polling day, I see only fourteen certificates and a few hundred digital files for lakhs of people like my husband's grandfather. 'Can apply' is not a certificate in hand. Didi promised no NRC in Bengal, and that promise has kept me sleeping at night. I do not trust papers and government forms—I have seen the NRC in Assam, I know what happens when you need documents the state says you don't have. The BJP is asking me to believe in a process that is barely delivering anything. I will vote for the woman who gave me things I can hold, and who promises to protect me from the one thing that truly frightens me.
primary_drivers: Material schemes already delivered (cycle, rice, healthcare)
Distrust of formal government bureaucratic processes for citizenship/papers
Mamata's 'no NRC in Bengal' promise vs. CAA's minimal delivery on the ground
